description: A race condition in MapData::MapData (src/compiler/heap-refs.cc) causes inconsistent caching of Map fields, leading to an out-of-bounds write during Maglev IR graph construction. The MapData constructor reads instance_size_ and in_object_properties_ from the live Map object in two separate non-atomic operations (lines 897 and 913). A concurrent modification of the Map's instance_size_in_words field (e.g., during in-object slack tracking of an Array map) between these two reads can produce an inconsistent state: instance_size_ captures the old smaller value while in_object_properties_ is computed from the newly-increased instance_size, yielding a value that implies more in-object properties than the cached instance_size can hold. When MaglevGraphBuilder::TryReadBoilerplateForFastLiteral uses this MapData, CreateJSArray allocates a VirtualObject with slot_count derived from the stale small instance_size_, but the subsequent loop iterates inobject_properties times (the inflated cached count), calling VirtualObject::set with offsets beyond the allocated slot array. This out-of-bounds write into ASAN-poisoned Zone memory constitutes a V8 sandbox violation. The bug is triggered by concurrently modifying an Array map's instance_size while Maglev compiles a function containing a fast array literal (VisitCreateArrayLiteral path), achievable via --jit-fuzzing --single-threaded --fuzzing flags.

fixes.evidence_text: Backport of CL 6993031/6999407: add SBXCHECK_LT for VirtualObject slot indices.
